This API endpoint allows you to retrieve records from a specified table. You can customize the response by applying various query parameters for filtering, sorting, and formatting.

**Pagination**: The response is paginated by default, with the first page being returned initially. The response includes the following pagination fields as top-level properties alongside `records`:

- **next**: URL to retrieve the next page of records. For example, `"https://app.nocodb.com/api/v3/tables/medhonywr18cysz/records?page=2"`. Returns `null` when on the last page.
- **prev**: URL to retrieve the previous page of records. Returns `null` when on the first page.
- **nestedNext**: URL to retrieve the current page of records with the next set of nested (linked record) data. Returns `null` when there are no more nested pages.
- **nestedPrev**: URL to retrieve the current page of records with the previous set of nested data. Returns `null` when on the first nested page.

These pagination fields are particularly valuable when working with large datasets divided into multiple pages. They provide the necessary URLs to seamlessly navigate forward and backward through the dataset.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Allows you to specify the fields that you wish to include from the linked records in your API response. By default, only Primary Key and associated display value field is included.

Example: `fields=["field1","field2"]` or `fields=field1,field2` will include only 'field1' and 'field2' in the API response.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Allows you to specify the fields by which you want to sort the records in your API response. Accepts either an array of sort objects or a single sort object.

Each sort object must have a 'field' property specifying the field name and a 'direction' property with value 'asc' or 'desc'.

Example: `sort=[{"direction":"asc","field":"field_name"},{"direction":"desc","field":"another_field"}]` or `sort={"direction":"asc","field":"field_name"}`

If `viewId` query parameter is also included, the sort included here will take precedence over any sorting configuration defined in the view.

parameters.name: where
parameters.description:
Enables you to define specific conditions for filtering records in your API response. Multiple conditions can be combined using logical operators such as 'and' and 'or'. Each condition consists of three parts: a field name, a comparison operator, and a value.

Example: `where=(field1,eq,value1)~and(field2,eq,value2)` will filter records where 'field1' is equal to 'value1' AND 'field2' is equal to 'value2'. 

You can also use other comparison operators like 'neq' (not equal), 'gt' (greater than), 'lt' (less than), and more, to create complex filtering rules.

If `viewId` query parameter is also included, then the filters included here will be applied over the filtering configuration defined in the view. 

Please remember to maintain the specified format, for further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
Enables you to control the pagination of your API response by specifying the page number you want to retrieve. By default, the first page is returned. If you want to retrieve the next page, you can increment the page number by one.

Example: `page=2` will return the second page of records in the dataset.

parameters.name: nestedPage
parameters.description:
Enables you to control the pagination of your nested data (linked records) in API response by specifying the page number you want to retrieve. By default, the first page is returned. If you want to retrieve the next page, you can increment the page number by one.

Example: `page=2` will return the second page of nested data records in the dataset.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Enables you to set a limit on the number of records you want to retrieve in your API response. By default, your response includes all the available records, but by using this parameter, you can control the quantity you receive.

Example: `pageSize=100` will constrain your response to the first 100 records in the dataset.

parameters.name: viewId
parameters.description:
***View Identifier***. Allows you to fetch records that are currently visible within a specific view. API retrieves records in the order they are displayed if the SORT option is enabled within that view.

Additionally, if you specify a `sort` query parameter, it will take precedence over any sorting configuration defined in the view. If you specify a `where` query parameter, it will be applied over the filtering configuration defined in the view. 

By default, all fields, including those that are disabled within the view, are included in the response. To explicitly specify which fields to include or exclude, you can use the `fields` query parameter to customize the output according to your requirements.

parameters.name: linksAsLtar
parameters.description: When set to `true`, Links fields will return full linked record data (id and display value) instead of just a count. This makes Links fields behave like LinkToAnotherRecord (LTAR) fields in the response.

post.description:
This API endpoint allows the creation of new records within a specified table. Records to be inserted are input as an array of key-value pair objects, where each key corresponds to a field name. Ensure that all the required fields are included in the payload, with exceptions for fields designated as auto-increment or those having default values. 

Certain read-only field types will be disregarded if included in the request. These field types include Look Up, Roll Up, Formula, Created By, Updated By, Created At, Updated At, Button, Barcode and QR Code.

For **Attachment** field types, this API cannot be used. Instead, utilize the storage APIs for managing attachments. Support for attachment fields in the record update API will be added soon.

patch.description:
This API endpoint allows you to update records within a specified table by their Record ID. The request payload should contain the Record ID and the fields that need to be updated.

Certain read-only field types will be disregarded if included in the request. These field types include Look Up, Roll Up, Formula, Created By, Updated By, Created At, Updated At, Button, Barcode and QR Code.

For **Attachment** field types, this API cannot be used. Instead, utilize the storage APIs for managing attachments. Support for attachment fields in the record update API will be added soon.

delete.description: This API endpoint allows the deletion of records within a specified table by Record ID. The request should include the Record ID of the record(s) to be deleted.

post.description: Find-or-create records in a single call. If a record matching the merge key exists, update it; otherwise insert a new one.

200.description: Upsert results with status per record

400.description: Bad request — invalid merge fields or missing records

parameters.name: linksAsLtar

parameters.name: where

Example: `where=(field1,eq,value1)~and(field2,eq,value2)` will filter records where 'field1' is equal to 'value1' AND 'field2' is equal to 'value2'. 

You can also use other comparison operators like 'neq' (not equal), 'gt' (greater than), 'lt' (less than), and more, to create complex filtering rules.

If `viewId` query parameter is also included, then the filters included here will be applied over the filtering configuration defined in the view. 

Please remember to maintain the specified format, for further information on this please see [the documentation](https://nocodb.com/docs/product-docs/developer-resources/rest-apis#v3-where-query-parameter)

parameters.name: linkFieldId
parameters.description: **Links Field Identifier** corresponding to the relation field `Link to another record` established between tables.

Example: `sort=[{"direction":"asc","field":"field_name"},{"direction":"desc","field":"another_field"}]` or `sort={"direction":"asc","field":"field_name"}`

Enables you to control the pagination of your API response by specifying the page number you want to retrieve. By default, the first page is returned. If you want to retrieve the next page, you can increment the page number by one.

Example: `page=2` will return the second page of linked records in the dataset.

parameters.name: pageSize

get.description:
Retrieve a list of documents in a base. Documents are returned without content for lightweight listing.

Use the `parent_id` query parameter to navigate the document hierarchy:
- `parent_id=null` or omitted — returns root-level documents
- `parent_id={docId}` — returns children of the specified document

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

parameters.name: parent_id
parameters.description: Parent document ID. Use `null` (or omit) for root-level documents, or a document ID to list its children.

post.description:
Create a new document in a base. If no `title` is provided, defaults to "Untitled". If no `content` is provided, defaults to an empty document.

To create a child document, pass the `parent_id` of the parent document.

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

content.text: We decided to use rich-text JSON as the canonical storage format for document content.
Content is stored as rich-text JSON, a structured document format that preserves rich-text formatting without lossy conversions.

get.description:
Retrieve a single document with its full content (rich-text JSON).

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

patch.description:
Update a document's title, content, or metadata.

**Optimistic concurrency control**: You must include the current `version` of the document. If the version does not match the server's version (i.e., someone else edited it), the request will be rejected with a 422 error. This prevents silent overwrites.

After a successful update, the returned document will have `version` incremented by 1.

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

content.text: New paragraph content added via API.

422.description: Version conflict — the document has been modified since it was last fetched. Fetch the latest version and retry.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a document. All child documents are also deleted (cascade).

Attachments (images, files) referenced by the document are also cleaned up.

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

parameters.name: docId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the document to delete.

patch.description:
Update a document's sort order among its siblings, and optionally move it to a different parent.

**Order**: Pass a float value. To insert between two siblings with orders 2.0 and 3.0, use 2.5 (midpoint).

**Move**: Include `parent_id` to re-parent the document. Pass `null` to move to root level. Circular moves (moving a document under itself or its descendants) are rejected.

Documents API is available on **Business** plan and above (Cloud) or with an **Enterprise** license (self-hosted).

parameters.name: docId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the document to reorder.

Change Sort Order.description: Move the document to position 2.5 (between siblings at 2.0 and 3.0).

Move to Different Parent.description: Move the document under a different parent and set its order.

Move to Different Parent Only.description: Move a document under a different parent (server assigns order).

Move to Root.description: Move a child document to the root level.

422.description: Invalid move — cannot move document under itself or its own descendant.

get.description:
Retrieve all comments for a specific record.

**Note**: Comment APIs are available only on self-hosted and cloud-hosted **Enterprise** plans.

post.description:
Create a new comment on a record.

**Note**: Comment APIs are available only on self-hosted and cloud-hosted **Enterprise** plans.

patch.description:
Update the text of an existing comment. Only the comment creator can update.

**Note**: Comment APIs are available only on self-hosted and cloud-hosted **Enterprise** plans.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a comment. Only the comment creator can delete.

**Note**: Comment APIs are available only on self-hosted and cloud-hosted **Enterprise** plans.

post.description:
Toggle the resolved state of a comment. If already resolved, unresolves it.

**Note**: Comment APIs are available only on self-hosted and cloud-hosted **Enterprise** plans.

fieldsToMergeOn.description: Field titles or IDs to match on (max 3). If a matching record exists it is updated; otherwise a new record is created. When multiple records match the same combination, the request is rejected.

records.description: Records to upsert — single object or array

id_fields.description: Composite PK values (when table has multi-column PK)

status.description: Whether this record was inserted or updated

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the base was last updated.

workspace_id.description: Unique identifier for the workspace to which this base belongs to.

id.description: Unique identifier for the data source.

type.description: Type of the data source (e.g., pg, mysql).

is_schema_readonly.description: Indicates if the schema in this data source is read-only.

is_data_readonly.description: Indicates if the data (records) in this data source is read-only.

Specifies the color of the base icon using a hexadecimal color code (e.g., `#36BFFF`).

**Constraints**:
- Must be a valid 6-character hexadecimal color code preceded by a `#`.
- Optional field; defaults to a standard color if not provided.

HookV3.description: Hook configuration for table-level event triggers.

event.description: Event trigger type. 'record' triggers after the operation completes.

active.description: Indicates if the hook is active.

trigger_fields.description: Field IDs that trigger the hook when modified.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the hook was last updated.

event.description: Event trigger type. 'record' triggers after the operation completes.

HookNotificationV3Url.title: URL
HookNotificationV3Url.description: URL webhook notification. Sends an HTTP request when triggered.

method.description: HTTP method for the webhook request.

path.description: URL to send the webhook to. Supports Handlebars templates.

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers to include in the request.

parameters.description: Query parameters to append to the URL.

value.description: Query parameter value. Supports Handlebars templates.

trigger_form.description: If true, the hook only fires when submitted via a specific form.

trigger_form_id.description: Form view ID that triggers this hook. Only applicable when trigger_form is true.

include_user.description: If true, includes the triggering user's info in the webhook payload.

HookNotificationV3Email.title: Email
HookNotificationV3Email.description: Email notification via configured email adapter.

to.description: Recipient email address. Supports Handlebars templates.

subject.description: Email subject line. Supports Handlebars templates.

trigger_form.description: If true, the hook only fires when submitted via a specific form.

trigger_form_id.description: Form view ID that triggers this hook. Only applicable when trigger_form is true.

include_user.description: If true, includes the triggering user's info in the webhook payload.

HookNotificationV3Script.title: Script
HookNotificationV3Script.description: Script notification. Executes a saved script when triggered.

scriptId.description: Identifier of the script to execute.

method.description: HTTP method used to invoke the script endpoint.

trigger_form.description: If true, the hook only fires when submitted via a specific form.

trigger_form_id.description: Form view ID that triggers this hook. Only applicable when trigger_form is true.

include_user.description: If true, includes the triggering user's info in the webhook payload.

HookNotificationV3Messaging.description: Messaging notification via plugin adapter (Slack, Discord, Telegram, etc.).

trigger_form.description: If true, the hook only fires when submitted via a specific form.

include_user.description: If true, includes the triggering user's info in the webhook payload.

table_id.description: Id of table associated with the view.

owned_by.description: User ID of the owner. Applicable only for personal views.

created_at.description: Timestamp of creation.

lock_type.description:
Lock type of the view.

 Note: Assigning view as personal using API is not supported currently

Lock type of the view.

show.description: Indicates whether the field should be displayed in the view.

width.description:
Width of the field in pixels.

 **Applicable only for grid view.**

aggregation.description:
Aggregation function to be applied to the field.

 **Applicable only for grid view.**

ViewFields.description:
List of fields to be displayed in the view. 

- If not specified, all fields are displayed by default.
- If an empty array is provided, only the display value field will be shown.
- In case of partial list, fields not included in the list will be excluded from the view.

mode.description: Mode of row coloring. In this mode, the color is selected based on conditions applied to the fields.
Mode of row coloring. In this mode, the color is selected based on a single select field.

field_id.description: Single select field ID to be used for colouring rows in the view.

apply_as_row_background.description: Whether to additionally apply the color as row background.

errorMessage.oneOf: Invalid row colour configuration. Must match one of the supported colour rule formats.

field_id.description: Identifier for the field being sorted.

direction.description: Direction of the group, either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending).

groups.description: List of groups to be applied on the grid view.

row_height.description: Height of the rows in the grid view.

field_id.description: Single select field ID to be used for stacking cards in kanban view.

stack_order.description:
Order of the stacks in kanban view. If not provided, the order will be determined by options listed in associated field.

Example: ```stack_order: ['option1', 'option2', 'option3']```

cover_field_id.description: Attachment field ID to be used as cover image in kanban view. If not provided, cover field configuration is skipped.

start_date_field_id.description: Date field ID to be used as start date in calendar view.

end_date_field_id.description: Date field ID to be used as end date in calendar view.

cover_field_id.description: Attachment field ID to be used as cover image in gallery view. Is optional, if not provided, the first attachment field will be used.

fk_geo_data_col_id.description: Foreign Key to GeoData Column to be used for the map view.

thank_you_message.description: Success message shown after form submission.

show_submit_another_button.description: Whether to show another form after submission.

reset_form_after_submit.description: Whether to show a blank form after submission.

form_hide_banner.description: Whether to hide the banner on the form.

logo.description: URL of the logo for the form.

send_response_email_to.description: Email address to send form responses to.

Note: Unique constraint is only supported for NC-DB (not external databases) and is mutually exclusive with default values. Map of widget IDs to their data.

type.description: 'count' counts all rows, 'summary' aggregates a column.

field_id.description: Column to aggregate. Required when type is 'summary'.

WidgetOptionsPieChart.description: Options for a pie chart widget (type=chart, chart_type=pie).

field_id.description: Column to aggregate. Required when type is 'summary'.

aggregation.description: Aggregation function. Required when type is 'summary'.

WidgetOptionsDonutChart.description: Options for a donut chart widget (type=chart, chart_type=donut). Same structure as pie chart.

WidgetOptionsBarChart.description: Options for a bar chart widget (type=chart, chart_type=bar).

sort_by.description: Sort by x-axis labels or y-axis values.

fields.description: Fields to aggregate on the y-axis.

WidgetOptionsLineChart.description: Options for a line chart widget (type=chart, chart_type=line). Same data structure as bar chart with additional line-specific appearance options.

smooth_lines.description: Smooth curve vs straight line segments.

WidgetOptionsText.description: Options for a text widget (type=text). Formatting is nested inside appearance.

dashboard_id.description: Unique identifier for the parent dashboard.

options.description: Widget configuration. Structure depends on widget type. See WidgetOptions* schemas.

x.description: Column position of the widget on the dashboard grid (0-indexed, left to right). e.g. 0 means the first column, 6 means starting at the 7th column.

y.description: Row position of the widget on the dashboard grid (0-indexed, top to bottom). e.g. 0 means the first row, 4 means starting at the 5th row.

w.description: Width of the widget in grid columns. e.g. 6 means the widget spans 6 columns (half of a 12-column grid).

h.description: Height of the widget in grid rows. e.g. 4 means the widget spans 4 rows tall.

position.description: Position and size of the widget on the dashboard grid.

table_id.description: Unique identifier for the associated table.

view_id.description: Unique identifier for the associated view.

error.description: Whether the widget has a configuration error.

title.description: Title of the document. Defaults to 'Untitled' if omitted.

meta.description: Arbitrary metadata (icon, cover image, settings, etc.).

parent_id.description: Parent document ID. null or omitted for root-level documents.

version.description: Current document version for optimistic concurrency control. Must match the server's version.

order.description: New sort order value (float). Compute midpoint between neighbors for fractional ordering.

parent_id.description: New parent document ID. null to move to root. Omit to keep current parent.

comment_count.description: Number of comments on this document.

DocumentListItem.description: Document summary returned in list responses. Excludes the `content` field.

record_id.description: Record ID this comment belongs to.

table_id.description: Table ID this comment belongs to.

created_by.description: User ID of the comment creator.

resolved_by.description: User ID who resolved this comment (null if not resolved).

parent_comment_id.description: Parent comment ID for threaded comments.

created_at.description: Creation timestamp.

CommentCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating a comment.

CommentUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating a comment.

CommentListResponse.description: List of comments for a record.

alias.description: Display label override for the field.

description.description: Help text shown below the field.

allow_scanner_input.description: Whether to show QR/barcode scanner input.

is_list.description: Whether to display as list layout.

validators.description: List of validators for the field.

FormFieldValidator.description: Validator configuration for a form field.

value.description: Validator parameter value (e.g., min/max number, pattern string).

message.description: Custom error message for validation failure.

regex.description: Regular expression pattern (for regex validator type).

created_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was last updated

updated_by.description: User ID of the last updater

WorkflowNodePort.description: Port definition for a workflow node (input/output connection point)

id.description: Unique port identifier within the node (e.g. 'true', 'false', 'body', 'output')

label.description: Display label for the port (e.g. 'True', 'False', 'For Each Item')

direction.description: Whether this port accepts incoming or outgoing connections

WorkflowVariableDefinition.description: Variable definition describing an input or output of a workflow node

groupKey.description: Grouping category — 'meta' for system fields, 'fields' for user data, 'iteration' for loop variables

isArray.description: Whether this variable represents an array of values

extra.description: Additional metadata (entity references, icons, UI type hints)

entity_id.description: ID of the referenced entity (column, table, or view)

port.description: Port ID this variable belongs to (for multi-port nodes like If/Iterate)

children.description: Nested variable definitions for object/array types

WorkflowNodeData.description: Data payload of a workflow node containing its configuration and metadata

config.description: Node-specific configuration. Structure varies by node type — e.g. table ID for record triggers, conditions array for If nodes, duration/unit for Delay nodes, array expression for Iterate nodes

testResult.description: Result from the most recent test execution of this node (only present on draft nodes)

inputVariables.description: Input variable definitions for this node

outputVariables.description: Output variable definitions produced by this node

WorkflowNode.description: A node in the workflow graph representing a trigger, action, or flow control operation. Node type IDs follow the pattern '{provider}.{category}.{name}' (e.g. 'nocodb.trigger.after_insert', 'core.flow.if', 'slack.send_message').

id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the workflow

type.description: Node type identifier determining behavior and available configuration. Categories: trigger (start events), action (operations), flow (control flow). Examples: 'nocodb.trigger.after_insert', 'core.action.http', 'core.flow.if', 'core.flow.iterate', 'core.flow.delay', 'slack.send_message', 'ai.action.generate-text'

position.description: Position of the node on the workflow canvas

targetPosition.description: Position of the incoming connection handle

sourcePosition.description: Position of the outgoing connection handle

WorkflowEdge.description: A directed connection between two workflow nodes defining execution flow. For conditional/loop nodes, sourcePortId determines which branch is followed.

animated.description: Whether the edge should be animated in the UI

label.description: Display label on the edge (e.g. 'True', 'False', 'For Each Item')

sourcePortId.description: Output port ID on the source node. Used for branching — e.g. 'true'/'false' for If nodes, 'body'/'output' for Iterate nodes

targetPortId.description: Input port ID on the target node

WorkflowNodeExecutionResult.description: Result of executing a single workflow node

nodeId.description: ID of the node that was executed

nodeTitle.description: Title of the node at execution time

input.description: Interpolated input data passed to the node

output.description: Output data produced by the node

error.description: Error message if the node failed

startTime.description: Execution start time (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

endTime.description: Execution end time (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

nextNode.description: Explicit next node title for routing (used by conditional nodes)

logs.description: Execution log entries from the node

metrics.description: Execution metrics (e.g. execution time, condition count)

inputVariables.description: Input variable definitions at execution time

outputVariables.description: Output variable definitions at execution time

loopContext.description: Loop context for iterate nodes — contains loop state, body port, and exit port

totalItems.description: Total number of items to iterate

bodyPort.description: Port ID for the loop body (e.g. 'body')

exitPort.description: Port ID for the loop exit (e.g. 'output')

isStale.description: Whether this test result is stale (node config changed since last test)

WorkflowLoopData.description: Execution data for a single iterate/loop node across all its iterations

iterations.description: Map of iteration index to iteration data. Each iteration contains its own nodeResults and optional nested childLoops.

nodeResults.description: Node execution results within this iteration

WorkflowExecutionData.description: Full execution state of a workflow run, including all node results, loop data, and pause/resume information

workflowId.description: ID of the workflow being executed

startTime.description: Execution start time (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

endTime.description: Execution end time (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

nodeResults.description: Ordered list of node execution results

currentNodeId.description: ID of the currently executing node (when status is 'running')

triggerData.description: Data that triggered the workflow execution

loops.description: Loop execution data keyed by iterate node ID

pausedAt.description: Timestamp when execution was paused (for delay/wait-until nodes)

nextNodeId.description: ID of the node to resume execution from

WorkflowDraft.description: Draft version of workflow nodes and edges with unpublished changes. Must be published via the publish endpoint for changes to take effect.

WorkflowNodeDataRequest.description: Data payload for a workflow node in create/update requests. Does not accept testResult — that field is server-managed.

config.description: Node-specific configuration. Structure varies by node type — e.g. table ID for record triggers, conditions array for If nodes, duration/unit for Delay nodes, array expression for Iterate nodes

inputVariables.description: Input variable definitions for this node

outputVariables.description: Output variable definitions produced by this node

WorkflowNodeRequest.description: A workflow node in create/update requests. Same as WorkflowNode but node data does not accept testResult.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the workflow

type.description: Node type identifier determining behavior and available configuration.

position.description: Position of the node on the workflow canvas

targetPosition.description: Position of the incoming connection handle

sourcePosition.description: Position of the outgoing connection handle

WorkflowDraftRequest.description: Draft version of workflow nodes and edges for create/update requests. Node data does not accept testResult.

WorkflowOptions.description: Workflow options including execution configuration and display settings

run_as.description: Execution identity — determines the permissions context under which the workflow runs

type.description: Identity type. 'service_account' runs as a system user with creator role, 'role' runs with a specified role, 'user' runs as a specific user

value.description: Role name (when type='role') or user ID (when type='user'). Not required for 'service_account'

icon.description: Custom emoji icon for the workflow

WorkflowGetResponse.description: Workflow with full details including nodes and edges

draft.description: Draft configuration with unpublished changes. Null when no draft exists.

options.description: Workflow options including execution configuration and display settings

created_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was last updated

updated_by.description: User ID of the last updater

trigger_data.description: Data to pass to the workflow trigger.

node_id.description: Unique identifier of the node to test.

test_trigger_data.description: Optional test data to pass to the node.

test_mode.description: Test mode. Defaults to sample_data if not specified.

position.description: Position of the node on the canvas.

targetPosition.description: Position of the incoming connection handle.

sourcePosition.description: Position of the outgoing connection handle.

WorkflowNodeListResponse.description: List of workflow nodes and edges

position.description: Position of the node on the canvas.

targetPosition.description: Position of the incoming connection handle. Defaults to 'top'.

sourcePosition.description: Position of the outgoing connection handle. Defaults to 'bottom'.

edges.description: Optional edges to add along with the node.

position.description: Position of the node on the canvas.

targetPosition.description: Position of the incoming connection handle.

sourcePosition.description: Position of the outgoing connection handle.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the execution record was created

execution_data.description: Full execution state including node results, loops, and pause/resume data

created_at.description: Timestamp when the execution record was created

message.example: Accessing api token api require enterprise plan

xc-token.name: Auth Token
xc-token.description: Auth Token is a JWT Token generated based on the logged-in user. By default, the token is only valid for 10 hours. However, you can change the value by defining it using environment variable `NC_JWT_EXPIRES_IN`.

bearerAuth.name: Authorization
bearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication. Use 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header format. This is an alternative to the xc-token header.

xc-token.name: xc-token
xc-token.description: API Token. Refer [here](https://docs.nocodb.com/account-settings/api-tokens/) to know more
